title: Bifrost: Unlocking Bitcoin DeFi on Cardano — Road to Mainnet (Phase 1 of 2)

rationale.summary: Vote: NO. For this TreasuryWithdrawals (active), downside risk and/or weak safeguards outweighed demonstrated benefit in the current evidence set. Decisive reason: a material claim is unsupported — "The bridge is already live on testnet under Catalyst Fund 14, with working peg-ins and peg-outs and participating SPOs." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit. Weighed against it: the proposal substantiates "Bifrost is designed as a permissionless Bitcoin-Cardano bridge secured by Cardano's SPO ecosystem that brings BTC onto Cardano as a native Cardano asset." and clears the evidence gates. What would change this vote to YES: Provide independent evidence for: The bridge is already live on testnet under Catalyst Fund 14, with working peg-ins and peg-outs and participating SPOs; Provide milestone-gated disbursement. Residual uncertainty: Treasury flow signal is in unsustainable regime, ratio 2.28 (total inflow (tau + donations) vs enacted withdrawals); advisory penalty applied. Confidence context: score -0.31; confidence 79.8%.
rationale.summary_raw: Doctrine-aware reasoning layer nudged the score by -0.030 (clamped to ±0.05): The proposal aligns with public-benefit ecosystem growth and could improve Cardano Bitcoin DeFi infrastructure, but the supplied claims show a large treasury ask with many high-materiality assertions about bridge security, testnet readiness, adoption targets, and treasury returns that are not backed by detailed evidence in the document. Given the deterministic assessment already marks claims and evidence as thin, only a small cautionary negative adjustment is justified rather than a stronger override.
top_fixes: Provide independent evidence for: The bridge is already live on testnet under Catalyst Fund 14, with working peg-ins and peg-outs and participating SPOs.

sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes

Requested: Phase 1 of 2 funding to take Bifrost from working testnet to launch readiness, including hardening, security audits, formal verification, ecosystem and partner readiness, stewardship, and economic foundations for launch.

Deliverables: Audited Bitcoin-Cardano bridge running on Cardano mainnet under controlled access, Support for both federated and SPO-threshold custody modes, Security audits and formal verification, Hardened economic model, Stewardship structure for launch, SPO and dApp partner pipeline required for public launch
Deadline/expiry: 9-month delivery period from July 2026 to March 2027

Claim (technical, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): Bifrost is designed as a permissionless Bitcoin-Cardano bridge secured by Cardano's SPO ecosystem that brings BTC onto Cardano as a native Cardano asset.
Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, high materiality): The bridge is already live on testnet under Catalyst Fund 14, with working peg-ins and peg-outs and participating SPOs.

Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal requests ₳12,332,031, approximately $1,973,125 at 0.16 USD/ADA, including a 10% refundable contingency, for a 9-month Phase 1 delivery period.

Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, high materiality): By the end of Phase 1, Bifrost will be an audited bridge running on Cardano mainnet in federated and SPO-threshold modes under controlled access.
Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): Public rollout and 24 months of operations are excluded from this request and are intended for a Phase 2 proposal in Q1 2027.

Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, high materiality): Bifrost custody of locked BTC is proposed to be distributed across 400+ Cardano SPOs weighted by delegation, with movement of funds requiring a cryptographic threshold representing the majority of delegated stake.
missing: Independent evidence for: The bridge is already live on testnet under Catalyst Fund 14, with working peg-ins and peg-outs and participating SPOs.
Independent evidence for: By the end of Phase 1, Bifrost will be an audited bridge running on Cardano mainnet in federated and SPO-threshold modes under controlled access.

Independent evidence for: Bifrost custody of locked BTC is proposed to be distributed across 400+ Cardano SPOs weighted by delegation, with movement of funds requiring a cryptographic threshold representing the majority of delegated stake.
Independent evidence for: The proposal says Bifrost has no bridge token, no founder allocation, and no built-in revenue claim for the founding teams beyond delivery compensation from this proposal.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
findings: Six-month treasury flow regime: unsustainable (basis: total inflow (tau + donations) vs enacted withdrawals)
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "Bifrost is designed as a permissionless Bitcoin-Cardano bridge secured by Cardano's SPO ecosystem that brings BTC onto Cardano as a native Cardano asset." and clears the evidence gates.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "The bridge is already live on testnet under Catalyst Fund 14, with working peg-ins and peg-outs and participating SPOs." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: if claims cannot be tied to replayable evidence, abstaining avoids overclaiming certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.
